name: Developments in Literacy
mission: Developments in Literacy (DIL) delivers transformative education to underserved children in Pakistan—especially girls—by operating high-quality schools, empowering local educators, and innovating technology solutions to ensure that every child succeeds.
programs: E-Libraries (Read to Grow, Read to Know)
theoryOfChange: DIL aims to deliver free high-quality education by combining technologically driven learning, rigorous teacher training, tailored curricula, and project-based activities so students can apply concepts to everyday life while expanding their imagination and opportunities beyond school.
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.dil.org/donate (Source: https://www.dil.org/donate)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Strong donor fit, low risk profile, and credible supporting evidence for outcomes.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 30/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: Website shows related EIN (33-0774111), likely parent/subsidiary of 33-0843213

score_details.score_summary: DEVELOPMENTS IN LITERACY INC shows exceptional impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.dil.org/donate (Source: https://www.dil.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: DIL holds a 100.0/100 score, an 89.8% program expense ratio, $0.04 fundraising efficiency, and 15.6 months of working capital.
The organization is zakat-eligible and provides information on zakat policy.
DIL reported total revenue of $2,785,991 and operates schools for underserved children.
The organization allocates 89.8% of its expenses toward program services.
DIL tracks student dropout rates and transitions from primary to secondary education.
The organization monitors Matric Board Exam pass rates as a metric for educational quality.
DIL offers various donation options including zakat-eligible giving methods.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: DIL earns a high overall rating due to its exceptional financial efficiency and high impact score of 48/50, though its alignment score is moderate at 30/50. The organization demonstrates high data confidence and clear evidence of capacity building in the global education sector.
areas_for_improvement: Deepening donor alignment to move from medium to high fit for specific philanthropic segments
Expanding vocational training pathways for graduates to further improve long-term economic impact

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium donor alignment with a score of 30/50, addressing the urgent global cause of education in Pakistan [1]. DIL is verified as zakat-eligible, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through its educational programs [2][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: DIL demonstrates high effectiveness through its Schools Program and Teacher Education initiatives, achieving a high impact score of 48/50 [1][6]. The organization maintains a low cost per beneficiary and high directness in its capacity-building efforts, supported by strong matriculation board exam pass rates and successful student transitions to higher education [6][8].

baseline_narrative.headline: Developments in Literacy (DIL) provides high-quality, free education to over 89,000 underserved children in Pakistan with a primary focus on female empowerment.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 89.8%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach students [1][5]
Highly efficient fundraising operations, spending only $0.04 to raise every $1 [1]
Strong liquidity with 15.6 months of working capital available to sustain operations [1]
baseline_narrative.summary: founded in 1997, Developments in Literacy (DIL) operates a network of 214 schools dedicated to providing quality education to underserved communities in Pakistan [1][3]. The organization maintains a perfect Charity Navigator score of 100.0/100, reflecting its commitment to financial transparency and accountability [1].

all_citations.quote: Transition from Primary to Middle and Middle to Secondary: 84

all_citations.claim: clarifying its specific zakat allocation policy
all_citations.quote: Improvement: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: DIL earns a high overall rating due to its exceptional financial efficiency and high impact score of 48/50, though its alignment score is moderate at 30/50. The organization demonstrates high data confidence and clear evidence of capacity building in the global education sector.

areas_for_improvement.context: While Zakat-eligible, DIL could improve by <cite id="16">clarifying its specific zakat allocation policy</cite> and eligible use categories for donors.
The organization currently operates in a well-covered space; <cite id="17">expanding to more remote or underserved geographies</cite> could increase its alignment score.

bbb_assessment.summary: Maintains perfect accountability scores across third-party rating platforms.

case_against.mitigation_notes: High reserves provide a critical safety net for operating in the volatile socio-political environment of Pakistan.
risk_factors: <cite id="13">Reserves significantly exceed the 6-12 month industry recommendation</cite>
case_against.summary: There are minimal concerns; however, the organization's <cite id="13">high working capital</cite> of 15.6 months may be viewed by some as excessive reserves.

confidence.data_freshness: 2023 Form 990 and 2024 Charity Navigator data used
confidence.notes: Data is highly consistent across multiple independent platforms.

known_gaps: Detailed staff count not in summary data

alignment.explanation: Strong fit for Muslim donors as a <cite id="20">Zakat-eligible organization</cite> serving <cite id="1">89,000+ students in Pakistan</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be strengthened by <cite id="16">detailing the specific asnaf categories</cite> served by their educational grants.

credibility.explanation: DIL is highly credible, holding a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and a <cite id="3">100.0/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To maximize credibility, DIL could <cite id="18">document and publish specific triggers</cite> for the deployment of its high reserves.

impact.explanation: Impact is exceptional, with a cost of only <cite id="19">$4.55 per beneficiary</cite> and a <cite id="6">89.8% program expense ratio</cite>.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Strong outcome tracking and verified metrics

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Funds local implementing partners to operate the school network

rich_narrative.headline: Developments in Literacy (DIL) provides high-quality, free education to over 89,000 underserved children in Pakistan with a primary focus on female empowerment.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize extreme financial efficiency and want to support long-term educational empowerment for girls in Pakistan.
donor_motivations: Want to see 89.8% of their donation go directly to program services
Value long-term academic outcomes like secondary school transition rates
Prefer established organizations with a 25+ year track record
Seek to fulfill Zakat through educational empowerment
giving_considerations: The organization has very high reserves, meaning immediate cash needs are low
Focus is exclusively on Pakistan, providing deep rather than broad geographic impact
Impact is delivered through a capacity-building model in local schools
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fund immediate emergency relief or those looking for a startup/high-growth organization with a low funding gap.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: DIL tracks <cite id="21">4 distinct outcome metrics</cite> over multiple years, including exam pass rates and dropout statistics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: DIL empowers communities by providing low-cost, high-quality education that prepares students for diverse professional fields.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking transition rates ensures that primary education actually leads to long-term economic opportunity.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite strong reserves, DIL's $2.8M revenue leaves a significant funding gap to reach millions of out-of-school children in Pakistan.

peer_comparison.differentiator: DIL distinguishes itself with a <cite id="6">89.8% program ratio</cite> compared to the <cite id="11">89.6% peer median</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on K-12 education in Pakistan
similar_organizations.name: Iqra Fund

similar_organizations.differentiator: Smaller scale with broader social services
similar_organizations.name: The Noor Project

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on orphans and vulnerable children
similar_organizations.name: Givelight Foundation

strengths.detail: DIL directs a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 89.8%</cite> toward its mission, significantly exceeding the <cite id="11">peer median of 89.6%</cite>.
The organization spends only <cite id="12">$0.05 to raise every $1</cite>, demonstrating highly efficient resource mobilization.
With <cite id="13">15.6 months of working capital</cite>, DIL possesses the financial resilience to sustain its school network through economic volatility.

strengths_deep_dive: Developments in Literacy stands out for its long-term commitment to student outcomes, evidenced by a <cite id="8">high transition rate to secondary education</cite> and a <cite id="14">79% rate of graduates proceeding to higher education</cite> or vocational training. Their governance is equally strong, featuring an <cite id="15">independent board of 14 members</cite> that ensures 89.8% compliance with accountability standards. This combination of academic success and rigorous oversight is validated by their <cite id="3">perfect Charity Navigator rating</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1997, Developments in Literacy (DIL) has established itself as a cornerstone of educational reform in Pakistan, operating a network of <cite id="1">214 schools serving 89,433 students</cite>. The organization focuses on providing high-quality, free education to underserved children, with a strategic emphasis on <cite id="2">female empowerment and teacher development</cite>. DIL maintains an exceptional financial profile, evidenced by a <cite id="3">perfect 100.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting top-tier accountability and transparency. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="4">total revenue of $2,440,822</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="5">$2.4 million in public contributions</cite>. Their operational model is remarkably lean, achieving a <cite id="6">89.8% program expense ratio</cite> in the most recent filing, ensuring that donor funds are directed almost entirely to educational services. Beyond basic literacy, DIL implements advanced initiatives such as <cite id="7">Ed-Tech (TEAL) and E-Libraries</cite> to modernize the learning experience for rural students. Their impact is quantifiable, with reports showing that <cite id="8">84% of students successfully transition</cite> from primary to middle and secondary education, while maintaining a <cite id="9">low student dropout rate of 5%</cite>. This sustained performance over nearly three decades has earned them the <cite id="10">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>, the highest level of recognition for nonprofit disclosure.
